channel.title: AI Viral News
channel.description: Curated viral AI news from Reddit + blogs — updated every 4 hours

item.title: [📰] Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
item.description:
Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four days using 48 of Nvidia's latest B200 graphics processors.

The model, called

item.title: [📰] Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description:
Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and a half, largely using Claude Code itself.

The launch marks a major inflection point i...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description:
Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise data, drafting documents, and taking action on behalf of employees.

The new Slackbot, now generally available to

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description:
Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a billboard in San Francisco displayi...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description:
The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive.

Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $20 to $200 per month depending on usage — has sparked a growing rebellion among the very programmers it aims...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description:
Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure.

TQ Ventures led the round, with participation from FPV Ventures...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Import AI 448: AI R&D; Bytedance’s CUDA-writing agent; on-device satellite AI
[📰] Import AI 450: China’s electronic warfare model; traumatized LLMs; and a scaling law for cyberattacks
[📰] Import AI 451: Political superintelligence; Google’s society of minds, and a robot drummer
[📰] Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting
[📰] Import AI 453: Breaking AI agents; MirrorCode; and ten views on gradual disempowerment
[📰] Import AI 454: Automating alignment research; safety study of a Chinese model; HiFloat4
[📰] Import AI 455: Automating AI Research
[📰] Import AI 456: RSI and economic growth; radical optionality for AI regulation; and a neural computer
[📰] How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines
item.description:
In a dimly lit bedroom, a frightened young woman is thrown onto a bed by a tall, muscular man. He grabs her hand, and flame-like vines crawl across her body, fusing with her flesh. She levitates, then drops. A dragon-shaped tattoo appears across her chest. “Two months,” the man says. “Give me an heir, or…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Musk v. Altman week 3: Elon Musk and Sam Altman traded blows over each other’s credibility. Now the jury will pick a side.
item.description:
Update: On Monday May 18, the jury sided with OpenAI, delivering an advisory verdict finding that Musk’s claims are barred by the statute of limitations. US District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers accepted the verdict. In the final week of the Musk v. Altman trial, lawyers traded blows over Elon Musk’s and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Import AI 457: AI stuxnet; cursed Muon optimizer; and positive alignment
[📰] What to expect from Google this week
item.description:
This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. When Google opens its doors tomorrow for its annual developer conference, I/O, it will do so as a clear third place in the foundation model race. A year ago, at Google I/O…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI
item.description:
On Monday, the jury in Musk v. Altman dealt Elon Musk a major blow—reaching a unanimous advisory verdict that he had sued OpenAI too late and, as a result, his claims are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations. US District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers immediately accepted it.  Musk announced on X that he will…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Roundtables: Inside the Musk v. Altman Trial
item.description:
Listen to the session or watch below Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI, in which he alleged CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman had deceived him over the company’s non-profit status. Watch as AI reporter and attorney Michelle Kim, who covered the trial for MIT Technology Review, joins in conversation with editor in…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] NVIDIA AI Releases Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion: A Tri-Mode Language Model with 6× Tokens Per Forward Over Qwen3-8B
item.description:
NVIDIA researchers have released Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion, a language model family that unifies three decoding modes in one architecture. The model supports autoregressive (AR) decoding, diffusion-based parallel decoding, and self-speculation decoding. It is available in 3B, 8B, and 14B parameter sizes. The family includes base, instruct, and vision-language variants. Sequential Decoding Limits Throughput Standard autoregressive (AR) language […]

The post

item.title: [📰] How to Build Knowledge Graph Generation Pipelines From Text With kg-gen, NetworkX Analytics, and Interactive Visualizations
item.description:
In this tutorial, we will generate knowledge graphs from plain text, conversations, and multiple source documents using kg-gen. We start by setting up the required dependencies and configuring an LLM through LiteLLM, then we extract entities, predicates, and relationships from simple text. As we move forward, we work with longer passages using chunking and clustering, […]

The post

item.title: [📰] Meet Turbovec: A Rust Vector Index with Python Bindings, and Built on Google’s TurboQuant Algorithm
item.description:
turbovec brings Google Research's TurboQuant algorithm to vector search, offering 16x compression and zero codebook training for RAG pipelines.

The post Meet Turbovec: A Rust Vector Index with Python Bindings, and Built on Google’s TurboQuant Algorithm appeared first on MarkTechPost.

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] What is a Forward Deployed Engineer: The AI Role OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Are Hiring in 2026
item.description:
OpenAI launched a $4B+ Deployment Company and Anthropic closed a $1.5B joint venture with Blackstone and Goldman Sachs — both built around the Forward Deployed Engineer model Palantir pioneered. Here is what FDEs actually do, why standard SaaS fails for enterprise AI, and what skills early-career AI engineers need to break into this role.

The post

item.title: [📰] With aluminum prices up 20%, recycling startups bet on AI to cash in
item.description:
Recycling startups are using AI to improve the recovery of critical minerals like aluminum, aiming to build a massive source of the metal.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Google is pitching an AI agent ecosystem to consumers who may not buy it
item.description:
One of the most promising introductions at Google’s I/O developer conference on Tuesday was a new way for consumers to use the web: AI agents. Unfortunately, it was also the most confusing.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Hark raises $700M Series A for its secretive ‘universal’ AI interface
item.description:
Hark expects to release its first multimodal models this summer, which it says will power a personal AI platform that works with existing products and services. The company expects to follow that with hardware devices built specifically for those systems.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] The Path, founded by Tony Robbins and Calm alums, hopes to offer safer AI therapy
item.description:
The Path says its AI model has scored 95 on the mental health safety AI benchmark, Vera-MH. This compares to a top score of 65 for the consumer bots. 

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
item.description:
The vibes were strong at Code with Claude, Anthropic’s two-day event for software developers in London that kicked off on May 19, the same day as Google’s I/O in Palo Alto. (A coincidence, not a flex, Anthropic staffers assured me.) “Who here has shipped a pull request in the last week that was completely written…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Spotify launches an ElevenLabs-powered audiobook creation tool
item.description:
The AI-powered audiobook generation won't bind authors to an exclusive contract, meaning they are free to publish their generated audiobooks anywhere.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Spotify adds AI-powered Q&A and briefing generation features to podcasts
item.description:
Spotify will let you generate daily or weekly briefs based on your prompts

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Trump delays AI security executive order, saying language ‘could have been a blocker’
item.description:
President Trump delayed signing an executive order that would have required pre-release government security reviews of AI models, citing dissatisfaction with the order's language.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Scaling creativity in the age of AI
item.description:
Storytelling is core to humanity’s DNA, stemming from our impulse to express ideals, warnings, hopes, and experiences. Technology has always been woven through the medium and the distribution: from early humans’ innovation of natural pigments and charcoals for cave paintings to literal representation by the camera. The landscape of storytelling continues to shift under our…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Six search engines worth trying now that Google isn’t really Google anymore
item.description:
Google is about to look really different, and if you're not a fan of the AI overview feature, then you're not going to like what's coming.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Spotify and Universal Music strike deal allowing fan-made AI covers and remixes
item.description:
Spotify is partnering with Universal Music Group￼ to let Premium subscribers create AI-generated song covers and remixes, with participating artists receiving a share of the revenue.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Roundtables: Can AI Learn to Understand the World?
item.description:
Listen to the session or watch below AI companies want to build systems that understand the external world and overcome the limitations of LLMs. Recent developments have brought world models to the forefront of the AI discussion. Watch a conversation with editor in chief Mat Honan, senior AI editor Will Douglas Heaven, and AI reporter…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] As Grok flounders, SpaceX bets future on beating Big Tech at AI
item.description:
SpaceX IPO filing pitches orbital data centers as Grok lags rival AI services.

📡 Ars Technica AI · 📍 Ars Technica AI

item.title: [📰] How CopilotKit Is Redefining the Agentic AI Stack in 2026
item.description:
An inside look at CopilotKit’s 2026 shipping cycle. Learn how the new AG-UI protocol, AIMock testing suite, and Pathfinder server are providing the production architecture developers need for agentic AI.

The post How CopilotKit Is Redefining the Agentic AI Stack in 2026 appeared first on MarkTechPost.

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] CODA: Rewriting Transformer Blocks as GEMM-Epilogue Programs
item.description:

item.title: [📰] Build Recurrent-Depth Transformers with OpenMythos for MLA, GQA, Sparse MoE, and Loop-Scaled Reasoning
item.description:
In this tutorial, we explore OpenMythos by building an advanced recurrent-depth transformer workflow that runs end-to-end in Google Colab. We create both MLA and GQA model variants, compare their parameter counts, and check the stability of the recurrent injection matrix through its spectral radius.

The post Build Recurrent-Depth Transfo...

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] Microsoft Releases Fara1.5: A Family of Browser Computer-Use Agents (4B/9B/27B) That Outperform OpenAI Operator and Gemini 2.5 Computer Use on Online-Mind2Web
item.description:
Microsoft Research released Fara1.5, a family of browser computer-use agents in 4B, 9B, and 27B sizes. Fara1.5-27B scores 72% on Online-Mind2Web, outperforming OpenAI Operator, Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, and Yutori Navigator n1. The release also includes FaraGen1.5, a synthetic data pipeline that trains agents on gated

The post

item.title: [📰] Steve Wozniak cheered after telling students they have AI – actual intelligence
item.description:
Steve Wozniak cheered after telling students they have AI – actual intelligence

item.title: [📰] Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
item.description:
During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the singularity is the theoretical future moment when AI rapidly exceeds human intelligence and dramatically transforms the world. But what struck me as I listened in the…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] AI put "synthetic quotes" in his book. But this author wants to keep using it.
